calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Rescinding Irrevoacable Trust, Declaring Trust Void Ab Initio, For Order Cancelling Deed Void Ab Initio And Declaring Title To Asse
Off Calendar Jun-11-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lisa Bryant for Andre Lee (Petitioner)

The Court noted it is inclined to grant the petition on its merits, but expressed concern that the petition leaves little protection to the beneficiary, Diana Lee. Ms. Bryant stated that parties have agreed Andre will deed the property back to his mother (Diana), then Diana is going to sell it, it is not in writing, and she can file a declaration stating the terms, and having it signed by both parties. Ms. Bryant also stated that she can file a notarized deed showing the property going back to the mother. The Court granted the petition for order rescinding irrevocable trust, etc., (subject to review of the order) and receipt of said declaration.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition For Order Rescinding Irrevoacable Trust, Declaring Trust Void Ab Initio, For Order Cancelling Deed Void Ab Initio And Declaring Title To Asse
Off Calendar Apr-09-2018 Continued To Apr-30-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Rescinding Irrevoacable Trust, Declaring Trust Void Ab Initio, For Order Cancelling Deed Void Ab Initio And Declaring Title To Asse
Off Calendar Mar-05-2018 Continued To Apr-09-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lisa Bryant for petitioner Andre Lee.

Ms. Bryant said that the irrevokable trust at issue had been created jointly by husband and wife to avoid Medical from being able to recover for medical care provided to the husband before his passing. She said that under a recent change of law it was not necessary for assets to be placed in an irrevokable trust to be protected, but would have the same protection if placed in a revokable trust. She said that the wife planned to sell the home, so she would no longer be in need of government benefits. Ms. Bryant contended that the wife had been under the mistaken belief that she would need to have the assets placed in an irrevokable trust for the duration of her lifetime; and that as she now wants to have access to the trust assets during her lifetime she should be allowed to eliminate the testamentary trust. She contended that there was no objection by any trustee or beneficiary to the proposed rescission of trust. The Court noted that he had not seen this type of request before, and said that he would like some authority on the issue. Ms. Bryant replied that she had successfully made similar requests in other counties, including Santa Clara, Santa Cruz and Orange, and that no such petition previously filed by her had been denied. The Court stated that he would continue the matter to give Ms. Bryant time to provide additional authority authorizing the requested relief. The matter was continued to April 9, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)
